images.caption: Figure 1:The illustration of our Q-ARVD framework.
Figure 2:Quantization sensitivity patterns in autoregressive video diffusion models, with scores normalized to sum to 1.

Figure 3:The outlier patterns in autoregressive video diffusion models. The x-axis denotes input channel index sorted in descending L2 norm, and the y-axis denotes the corresponding L2 norm values. Outlier channels identified by our method (§3.2,Equation˜12) are highlighted in red. In practice, we further align the number of detected outlier channels to a multiple of 32 for hardware-friendly deployment. Additional samples are provided in the Appendix §A.
Figure 4:The ratio of layers containing outliers in terms of layer type and block depth. A layer is identified as outlier-containing upon detection of at least one outlier channel.

Figure 5:The visual comparison of the self-forcing model with
W4A8. Additional samples are presented in the Appendix (§BFigure˜11-Figure˜16).
Figure 6:The sensitivity to thresholdτ\tauof Modified Z-score using self-forcing.
Figure 6:The sensitivity to thresholdτ\tauof Modified Z-score using self-forcing.
Figure 7:The coefficient of variation, bitwidth-order agreement and discriminability score of each metric. Use data ofTable˜1.
